title: Earnings Volatility Watch: This Stock Could Move 30% In A Single Day This Week
author: Piero Cingari
description: First-quarter 2026 earnings season opens with unusually high stakes following the U.S.-Iran war's impact on oil markets and supply chains. Major banks dominate the calendar, with focus on loan demand and credit stress. Options markets are pricing single-digit to nearly 30% post-earnings swings across 10 large-cap companies, with Wipro showing the most extreme volatility expectations at 30.09% implied move.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: EPS expected to fall 29.91% year-over-year as higher energy input costs bite into margins; revenues also declining 2.99%. Results will reveal impact of trade tariffs and energy cost pressures on commodity-linked manufacturers.
Strong EPS growth of 62.28% expected, but company faces elevated credit loss pressure from used car value repricing. Mixed signals with positive earnings growth offset by ongoing credit challenges.

title: One Fund Sold $21 Million in Vipshop Stock as E-Commerce Giant Posts $3 Billion in Revenue
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: North of South Capital reduced its Vipshop stake by 1.09 million shares ($21.04 million) in Q4, cutting the position from 6.3% to 3.6% of assets. Despite the sale, the fund retained a $40 million stake. Vipshop reported Q3 revenue of $3 billion (up 3.4% YoY) and net income growth of 16.8%, with guidance for up to 5% revenue growth next quarter, suggesting the trim was tactical rather than a loss of confidence.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed signals: fund reduced position significantly (tactical trim), but retained substantial $40M stake and company shows solid fundamentals with 3.4% revenue growth, 16.8% net income growth, and positive forward guidance. Stock up 20.9% YoY. The reduction appears strategic rebalancing rather than loss of confidence.
Largest holding in North of South Capital's portfolio at $220.18M (19.8% of AUM), indicating strong conviction in this emerging market financial services play.
Second-largest holding at $137.90M (12.4% of AUM) in a fund focused on emerging market and Asia tech names, reflecting confidence in semiconductor sector exposure.
Third-largest holding at $122.06M (11.0% of AUM), demonstrating continued conviction in Chinese e-commerce and tech sector despite broader portfolio rebalancing.

title: YMM Stock Fell Nearly 20% Last Quarter, but One Fund Is Betting $116 Million on a Turnaround
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Despite Full Truck Alliance (YMM) stock falling nearly 20% in Q4 2025, London-based North of South Capital LLP increased its position by 240,446 shares, bringing its total stake to $116.40 million (10.46% of AUM). The fund's confidence appears grounded in the company's operational strength, with Q3 2025 showing 10.8% revenue growth and 22.3% order growth, despite near-term market skepticism around China tech stocks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed signals: stock declined 18.8% over the past year and fell nearly 20% last quarter, reflecting market skepticism around China tech. However, operational metrics remain strong with 10.8% revenue growth, 22.3% order growth, and 39% transaction service revenue surge in Q3 2025. The company maintains solid fundamentals with $4.4 billion in cash and positive net income, suggesting the decline is driven by sentiment rather than operational distress.
Mentioned as a top holding of North of South Capital ($220.18 million, 19.8% of AUM), indicating institutional confidence, but no specific performance data or news provided in the article.
Listed as a significant holding of North of South Capital ($137.90 million, 12.4% of AUM), but no specific performance data or news provided in the article.
Mentioned as a top holding of North of South Capital ($122.06 million, 11.0% of AUM), but no specific performance data or news provided in the article.

title: JD.com Stock Tanked Nearly 20% Last Quarter, but One Fund Still Bought Up $6 Million in Shares
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: North of South Capital LLP purchased 180,081 shares of JD.com worth approximately $5.63 million during Q4 2025, despite the stock declining nearly 20% that quarter. The fund's total JD.com position stands at $122.06 million (11% of assets), representing conviction in the company despite recent underperformance. While JD.com's stock is down 28.7% over the past year, the company showed operational strength with 14.9% revenue growth and 700+ million active customers, though margin pressures from increased marketing and fulfillment costs remain a concern.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed signals: negative near-term performance (down 28.7% YoY, 20% decline last quarter) and margin pressures from heavy investment spending, but offset by positive operational metrics (14.9% revenue growth, 30.8% service revenue growth, 700M+ active customers) and institutional buying conviction during the drawdown. The depressed valuation could present opportunity if margins stabilize.

Mentioned as a top holding of North of South Capital (12.4% of AUM, $137.90 million position) with no specific news or performance commentary in the article. Neutral mention as context for fund portfolio composition.
Mentioned as North of South Capital's largest holding (19.8% of AUM, $220.18 million) with no specific news or performance analysis. Neutral mention as context for fund portfolio composition.

title: Amazon Pay Competitor Profile 2025: A Journey from 'Pay with Amazon' to a Leading Payment Platform
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: Amazon Pay, originally launched in 2007 as Pay with Amazon, has expanded its payment solutions beyond online transactions to include in-store, peer-to-peer, credit, and loan services. The report provides insights into Amazon Pay's business model, product offerings, and strategic milestones, including partnerships and collaborations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The partnership between Amazon Pay and ICICI Bank to launch a Visa-branded credit card is seen as a positive development for the company.
